codeReview.summary: Documentation fix correctly removes false npm install claims from README.md Step 4 and accurately documents that the linter is invoked via 'node tooling/ui-md-linter/ui-md-lint.js'. No npm install or npx claims remain in README.md.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No code changes were made - purely a documentation fix. No convention gaps to report.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker correctly identified the issue was documentation-only, not a publish-to-npm solution

sharedStateObservations.observation: docs-worker skill procedure was followed correctly. Worker verified npm install fails (404), confirmed README was the only file with false claims, and updated documentation accurately.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows grep for 'npm install.*ui-md-linter|npx ui-md-linter' returned no matches in README.md after fix

sharedStateObservations.observation: No services or commands were started that should be in services.yaml. Linter was invoked locally as documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Verification commands ran linter directly via node, matching the documented invocation

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new knowledge gaps discovered. The linter's non-published-to-npm status is already implicit in the codebase structure (no package.json in tooling/ui-md-linter that publishes to npm).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: tooling/ui-md-linter/ has no npm publish configuration

validationChecks.detail: Package does not exist on npm (404 Not Found). This is expected and acceptable per VAL-DOCS-002 option B.
README.md Step 4 now correctly documents 'node tooling/ui-md-linter/ui-md-lint.js ./UI.md' with explanation that no global install is required.

validationChecks.check: No false npm install claims remain in README.md
validationChecks.detail: grep for 'npm install.*ui-md-linter|npx ui-md-linter' returns no matches in README.md
Linter runs successfully with 'node tooling/ui-md-linter/ui-md-lint.js ./UI.md' and all 3 sample files pass validation

summary: Feature PASS. The worker correctly chose option B of VAL-DOCS-002: since npm install -g ui-md-linter does not work (package not published to npm), the README.md was updated to accurately document the actual invocation method 'node tooling/ui-md-linter/ui-md-lint.js'. False npm install and npx claims were removed. The linter was verified to work correctly with the documented invocation. No blocking issues found.
